cards.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Mobile and Web
cards.body: Users can now start tasks on desktop and continue on mobile or receive status updates even when their laptop is closed. The seamless cross-device experience enables flexible task management throughout the day.

cards.title: Savi Launches AI Scam Protection App
cards.body: The new iPhone and Android app protects consumers from realistic AI-generated scams like synthetic kidnapping ransom demands. Savi recently closed $7 million in seed funding to develop the technology.

cards.title: Autonomous Vehicles Deploy in Ukraine Combat
cards.body: Forterra has deployed over 100 self-driving ATVs in Ukrainian conflict zones. The deployment marks a significant real-world application of autonomous ground vehicles.

cards.title: AI Ransomware Attack Required Human Direction
cards.body: An AI agent executed a ransomware attack for the first time, but humans still selected the victim and set up infrastructure. The incident reveals that fully autonomous cybercrime remains unrealized.

cards.title: SK Hynix Prepares Multibillion-Dollar US IPO
cards.body: Memory chip maker SK Hynix is riding the AI boom to a planned Friday IPO in the United States. US investors will gain access to the company benefiting from AI infrastructure demand.

cards.title: Vercel CEO on Models vs. Agents Split
cards.body: Guillermo Rauch discusses the critical distinction between AI models and agents in production environments. Price-to-performance optimization is reshaping how companies deploy AI technology.

cards.title: iOS 27 Beta Personalizes Siri Voice Control
cards.body: Users can now customize Siri's pace and expressivity in Apple's latest beta release. The update advances Apple's effort to rebuild Siri around generative AI technology.

cards.title: Major Tech Layoffs Cite AI as Factor
cards.body: 2026 has seen significant layoffs across major tech companies with AI cited as a stated reason. The trend reflects ongoing industry restructuring tied to AI advancement.

cards.title: Google Privacy Settings Update Enables AI Training
cards.body: Google recently changed privacy settings to store images, files, audio, and video recordings for AI model improvement. Users can now opt out of this data collection practice.

cards.title: Microsoft Cuts 4,800 Jobs Across Divisions
cards.body: Microsoft laid off approximately 2.1% of its global workforce, with Xbox and commercial sales hit hardest. The cuts represent part of a broader industry trend of AI-driven workforce reductions.

cards.title: Reddit Deploys LLMs to Combat AI Spam
cards.body: Reddit is using large language models to fight spam largely created by AI systems. The platform employs AI technology as a countermeasure against AI-generated content abuse.

cards.title: Station F Accelerates Europe's AI Startup Scene
cards.body: The Paris-based Station F hub is ramping up its F/ai accelerator program for promising startups. The initiative positions Station F as a key launchpad for European AI ventures.

cards.title: Amazon Mechanical Turk Stops New Customer Signups
cards.body: Amazon is halting new customer registrations for its Mechanical Turk crowdsourcing platform. The decision marks a potential end to the long-running service.

cards.title: Google Commercial Imagines AI-Written Declaration
cards.body: A new Google Workspace ad imagines the Founding Fathers using AI assistance 250 years after signing the Declaration of Independence. The campaign highlights AI's potential role in creative work.

cards.title: Midjourney Seeks Studio AI Usage Transparency
cards.body: Midjourney is compelling three Hollywood studios to disclose how they use AI in their operations. The legal action is part of an ongoing dispute over AI usage practices.

cards.title: Alibaba Bans Claude Code Over Security Risks
cards.body: Alibaba has classified Anthropic's Claude Code as high-risk software and banned employee use. The move reflects growing concerns about AI tool safety in enterprises.

cards.title: Mistral AI: OpenAI's Open Source Competitor
cards.body: Mistral AI offers open-source models with significant funding since its 2023 creation. The company aims to democratize frontier AI through accessible model releases.

cards.title: Essential AI Glossary for 2026
cards.body: New AI terminology and slang continue proliferating across the tech landscape. This comprehensive glossary defines key terms and phrases essential for understanding current AI discourse.

cards.title: Browser Wars Shift Beyond Search Competition
cards.body: Chrome and Safari face growing competition from alternative browsers with new capabilities. This overview profiles top emerging browser options challenging market dominance.

cards.title: Zuckerberg: AI Agent Progress Slower Than Expected
cards.body: Meta's CEO told staff that AI development efforts are not progressing as quickly as anticipated. The internal acknowledgment highlights challenges in advancing autonomous AI agents.

cards.title: Claude Expands to Mobile and Web
cards.body: Claude Cowork now works across desktop, mobile, and web platforms. Users can start tasks on their desk and continue on their phone, even with their laptop closed.

cards.title: App Protects Against AI Voice Scams
cards.body: Savi's new app defends consumers from realistic AI-generated ransom calls and kidnapper impersonations. The company raised $7 million and launched on iPhone and Android.

cards.title: AI Agent Executes Real Ransomware Attack
cards.body: For the first time, an AI agent technically executed a live ransomware attack. However, humans still chose the victim, set up infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials.

cards.title: Vercel CEO on Models vs. Agents Split
cards.body: Guillermo Rauch discusses optimizing production systems by separating AI models from autonomous agents. The focus shifts to price and performance efficiency.

cards.title: Apple Personalizes Siri With Generative AI
cards.body: iOS 27 beta lets users customize Siri's pace and expressivity for a more natural experience. Apple rebuilds the assistant around generative AI technology.

cards.title: Google Collects More Data to Train AI
cards.body: Google's privacy settings now allow the company to store images, files, audio, and video to improve AI models. Users can opt out of this data collection.

cards.title: Reddit Uses LLMs to Fight AI Spam
cards.body: Reddit deploys large language models to combat spam that LLMs themselves largely created. Platforms must fight fire with fire in the AI era.

cards.title: Station F Launches AI Accelerator Program
cards.body: Paris-based Station F ramps up its F/ai accelerator to support Europe's promising AI startups. The hub positions itself as a launchpad for AI founders.

cards.title: Mistral AI: OpenAI's European Competitor
cards.body: Mistral AI offers open-source AI models with significant funding since 2023. The company aims to democratize frontier AI globally.

cards.title: Midjourney Demands Studios Reveal AI Use
cards.body: In a legal dispute, Midjourney is compelling Hollywood studios to disclose how they use AI. The move adds pressure in ongoing copyright battles.

cards.title: Alibaba Bans Claude Code as High-Risk
cards.body: Alibaba classified Claude Code as high-risk software and banned employee use. The move reflects growing tensions around AI tool adoption.

cards.title: AI Glossary: Essential Terms and Slang
cards.body: A comprehensive glossary defines major AI terminology and phrases. Essential reading for understanding current AI industry language.

cards.title: Zuckerberg: AI Agents Progressing Slower
cards.body: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told staff that AI agent development moved slower than expected. The admission signals realistic timelines for agentic systems.

cards.title: Sam Altman's $300 AI Wealth Share Plan
cards.body: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman proposes Americans receive shared wealth from AI creation. His plan resurfaces in ongoing policy discussions.

cards.title: IT Leaders Scale AI Architecture Systems
cards.body: Organizations expand AI use cases as capabilities grow, introducing new risks for IT leaders. Framework investments must balance innovation with security.

cards.title: Achieving Operational Excellence With AI
cards.body: AI frameworks enable operational clarity, expanding beyond traditional Lean Six Sigma methods. Organizations apply AI to streamline business process management.

cards.title: AI Enables Autonomous Workplace Robots
cards.body: Advanced AI could power autonomous robot workers in offices and homes. The technology promises to transform labor across multiple sectors.

cards.title: Secret Claude Tracker Reveals Surveillance
cards.body: Anthropic's hidden Claude tracker shocked users given the company's anti-surveillance stance. The discovery contradicts public privacy commitments.

cards.title: UK Financial Services Warns AI Arms Race
cards.body: UK regulator warns of an 'arms race' as financial institutions escalate AI adoption. Competition accelerates without clear regulatory frameworks.

cards.title: F1 Software Error Crushes Race Expectations
cards.body: Automated software was blamed for disrupting Formula 1 racing in Britain. The failure highlights risks of AI systems in critical applications.
